questions.question: What is the unit of force?
Which equation relates force, mass, and acceleration?
What does the symbol F represent?
What happens to an object's acceleration if the force acting on it increases but its mass stays the same?
A shopping trolley has a mass of 20 kg. If it accelerates at 3 m/s², what force is applied?
If the same force is applied to two objects, which one accelerates more?
options: The object with the greater mass.
The object with the smaller mass.

questions.question: What is the mass of an object if a force of 50 N causes it to accelerate at 5 m/s²?
A force of 80 N acts on a 20 kg object. What is its acceleration?
If the mass of an object doubles while the force stays the same, the acceleration will:
Which statement is true?
options: More force causes greater acceleration if mass stays constant.
Force and mass are measured in the same units.

questions.question: A force of 24 N is applied to a 6 kg object. What is the acceleration?
An object accelerates at 8 m/s² when a force of 40 N is applied. What is its mass?
Which of the following would require the greatest force to accelerate?
A 10 kg object experiences a force of 100 N. If the force is increased to 200 N, the acceleration becomes:
Which change would cause the greatest increase in acceleration?
options: Increasing both force and mass equally.

questions.question: A rugby player pushes with a force of 600 N on a scrum. If the opposing force is also 600 N, what is the acceleration?
A force of 90 N acts on a box with a mass of 30 kg. What is the acceleration?
A 12 kg object accelerates at 5 m/s². What is the applied force?
A car's engine provides a greater driving force while its mass stays constant. The car will:
Two objects experience the same force. Object A has twice the mass of Object B. Compared with Object B, Object A will have:
Which quantity is measured in kilograms (kg)?
What happens to acceleration if the force on an object is halved but the mass stays the same?
A 4 kg object is pushed with a force of 20 N. What is its acceleration?
A force of 36 N produces an acceleration of 6 m/s². What is the object's mass?
Which object would need the least force to accelerate?
Which statement about force is correct?
options: Force can only slow objects down.
Force can change an object's motion.

questions.question: A 15 kg object accelerates at 2 m/s². What force is applied?
A force of 72 N acts on a 9 kg object. What is its acceleration?
If an object's mass is reduced while the same force is applied, the acceleration will:
Which of these is an example of a force?
A force of 45 N acts on an object with a mass of 15 kg. What is its acceleration?
A 5 kg object accelerates at 6 m/s². What force is acting on it?
Which variable is measured in m/s²?
If both force and mass are doubled, the acceleration will:
A truck has a much greater mass than a bicycle. If both experience the same force, the truck will:
A force of 56 N causes an object to accelerate at 7 m/s². What is its mass?
A 25 kg object accelerates at 4 m/s². What is the force?
Which situation shows the greatest acceleration?
If no net force acts on an object, it will:
A force of 18 N is applied to a 3 kg object. What is the acceleration?
